summary: 2026-06-06 16:05:12 PDT — Verdict: the best fit is H2, but more specifically an SDK response-shape bug in this repo. The bundled type says `getRunningContext()` returns `{ context: RunningContext }`, while `Root` reads `context?.runningContext`; the mock/tests use the non-SDK shape, so a real `inCamera` instance would route as `panel` and never mount `OverlayApp`, exactly matching zero `drawParticipant`/`drawWebView` logs. (1) `runRenderingContext({ view: 'camera' })` likely opens a separate rendering instance: `onRenderedAppOpened` explicitly says the inMeeting instance is notified that a second instance opened for Layers API rendering, despite ambiguous `runRenderingContext` prose. H1 is unlikely. (2) The draw calls belong in the camera/rendering instance after config/init/mount; `onRenderedAppOpened` is panel-side readiness/diagnostic coordination, not the camera component’s draw trigger. The sample shape is consistent with the types, assuming Marketplace camera capabilities/surface config are correct. (3) The `connect` error is not the current no-draw cause because `connect()` is swallowed and cannot trigger Root’s fallback; it may still cause a blank overlay after drawing if the state bridge fails. (4) The single best diagnostic is to log, per page-load/instance id before routing, both `config().runningContext` and the raw `getRunningContext()` response; also log `Root` init catches and `onRenderedAppOpened`. (5) `renderTarget` and `participantUUID` wiring are broadly valid, but `drawParticipant` failure currently aborts `drawWebView`, which can still hide the overlay once routing is fixed.

findings.title: Real running context is read from the wrong property
findings.claim: `RealZoom.init()` returns the raw result of `sdk.getRunningContext()` (`client/src/zoom/zoomAdapter.js:214`), but the bundled SDK type defines `RunningContextResponse` as `{ context: RunningContext }` (`sdk.d.ts:1101`). `Root` then reads `context?.runningContext`, so real contexts resolve to `undefined`; `renderModeFor(undefined)` returns panel, so the camera instance never mounts `OverlayApp` and never calls the draw path.
findings.suggestion: Normalize the SDK result in the adapter or Root, e.g. derive `runningContext = raw?.runningContext ?? raw?.context ?? cfg?.runningContext`, return one canonical shape, and route on that canonical value.

findings.title: Tests and mocks encode the non-SDK context shape
findings.claim: The fake SDK returns `{ runningContext: 'inMeeting' }`, and `MockZoom.init()` does the same shape at `client/src/zoom/zoomAdapter.js:83`. That masks the real SDK contract and lets the Root/router bug survive tests.
findings.suggestion: Change fakes/mocks to exercise the real `{ context: 'inMeeting' | 'inCamera' }` shape, and add a routing/adapter test proving a real-shaped `inCamera` response mounts overlay mode.

findings.title: drawParticipant failure prevents drawWebView
findings.claim: `drawCameraOverlay()` awaits `drawParticipant` before `drawWebView`; if `drawParticipant` rejects, the webview overlay is never drawn. The SDK types show `drawParticipant` is Host/Co-Host only (`sdk.d.ts:4724`), while `drawWebView` supports broader roles (`sdk.d.ts:4824`), so a base-layer permission/capability failure can unnecessarily suppress the meter.
findings.suggestion: Log/catch `drawParticipant` failures and continue to `drawWebView`; treat the participant base layer as optional unless the product explicitly requires host/co-host-only overlay behavior.

findings.title: Rendered-instance event and Marketplace surface still need live confirmation
findings.claim: The SDK exposes `onRenderedAppOpened` specifically to notify the inMeeting instance that a second Layers rendering instance opened (`sdk.d.ts:5254`), but the app neither requests nor registers that event. The types support the second-instance model, but the live run still needs to confirm the raw context value, event firing, and Marketplace camera-mode surface/capability setup.
findings.suggestion: For the next diagnostic run, add `onRenderedAppOpened` to requested/Marketplace capabilities, register a panel-side handler, and log its payload alongside raw `config().runningContext`, raw `getRunningContext()`, `OverlayApp` mount, draw entry, and `Root` init catch.
